title: quadlet: error creating container storage: the container name is already in use after host reboot
body:
### Issue Description

Any rootful pods run from systemd services generated by quadlet files that have `WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target`, fail to start on host reboot with:
```
Error: creating container storage: the container name "nginx-nginx" is already in use by 46e0844f58cb0d923b1921ad7d7740f862fcbc4491a927e4e602d02a3a801cb5. You have to remove that container to be able to reuse that name: that name is already in use
``` 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

The issue can be easily reproduced with the following steps:
1. Spin up a vanilla AlmaLinux 9.3 VM from the respective generic cloud images
2. Install podman
3. Add the following quadlet files:
```
[user@test ~]$ ls -lah /etc/containers/systemd/
total 8.0K
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root  42 Apr 18 18:11 .
drwxr-xr-x. 8 root root 170 Apr 18 18:09 ..
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 197 Apr 18 18:11 nginx.kube
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 200 Apr 18 18:09 nginx.yaml
[user@test ~]$ for f in /etc/containers/systemd/*; do echo $f; cat $f; echo; done
/etc/containers/systemd/nginx.kube
[Unit]
Description=The nginx pod
Before=local-fs.target

[Kube]
Yaml=nginx.yaml
PublishPort=80:80

[Service]
Restart=always
TimeoutStartSec=900

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

/etc/containers/systemd/nginx.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx
  labels:
    app: nginx
spec:
  containers:
    - name: nginx
      image: nginx:latest
      ports:
        - name: http
          containerPort: 80
```
4. Reload systemd daemon using `sudo systemctl daemon-reload` to generate the respective systemd service from the quadlet files, and start the service using `sudo systemctl start nginx`
5. Check that the service is running and is functional, and perform a reboot in the host vm (the one that the above container is currently running).

### Describe the results you received

There is a very good chance (not always) that the systemd service that corresponds to the quadlet files will fail to start. Check the system logs and should be like this:
```
[user@test ~]$ sudo journalctl -u systemd -u podman -u nginx

Apr 18 18:14:26 test systemd[1]: Starting The nginx pod...
```

### Describe the results you expected

The podman pod that correspond to the quadlet files to automatically start without errors on reboot.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

The issue can be resolved using the following steps:
1. Stop the failing systemd service using `sudo systemctl stop nginx` 
2. Run `sudo podman ps --external` and get the failing container id
3. Run `sudo podman rm --storage <container-id>` to delete the malfunctioning container
4. Start again the relevant systemd service using `sudo systemctl start nginx`

comments.body: Possibly relevant to issues #16476, #19521, #20021 but with a clear reproducible case
Please use a newer podman, 4.6 had problem with c/storage container removal so I am pretty sure this works on newer podman versions.
